a11y.formInvalid: The form contains errors. Please review the highlighted fields.

header.loginNotAvailable: Sign in is not yet available.

home.title: Create and manage policies
home.description: Create, review and maintain policies for the Construct-X dataspace.

orientation.title: Your next sensible step
orientation.description: Start with a new policy or review existing ones before sharing data with partners.

useCases.label: Use Cases
useCases.description: Use cases of the Construct-X dataspace

empty.title: No policies yet
empty.description: Create your first policy to see it here.

policies.title: Policies
policies.subtitle: All policies maintained in this hub

empty.title: No policies yet
empty.description: Create your first policy to clearly govern data sharing and usage.
empty.hint: Or start later from the overview

error.title: Policies could not be loaded
error.description: The overview is currently unavailable. Please retry or return to the home page.

policyDetail.alwaysTrue: This policy has no constraints — it allows everything.

subtitle.create: Define a new policy with a unique Policy ID, category and constraints.
subtitle.edit: Edit the policy and its constraints.

sections.categoryHint: Choose whether the policy governs access to or usage of the data.
sections.constraintsHint: Add one or more constraints. Multiple constraints are combined.

policyId.label: Policy ID
policyId.hint: Choose a unique ID without spaces (e.g. geodata-access-2027).

palette.title: Add constraint
palette.subtitle: Choose from the constraints available for the selected category.

constraint.empty: No constraints added yet. Without constraints this policy allows everything.

MEMBERSHIP.hint: The consumer must be an active member of the dataspace. No further configuration required.

USE_CASE.label: Use cases
USE_CASE.hint: Select one or more use cases.

END_DATE.label: Valid until
END_DATE.hint: Choose a date in the future.

FRAMEWORK_AGREEMENT.hint: The consumer must have agreed to this framework agreement:

ACCESS.label: Access Policy
ACCESS.description: Governs who is allowed to access the data.

CONTRACT.label: Contract Policy
CONTRACT.description: Governs under which conditions the data may be used.

MEMBERSHIP.label: Membership
MEMBERSHIP.legalText: The data consumer must be an active, verified member of the Construct-X dataspace at the time of data access. Access by non-members or organizations with suspended membership is excluded.

USE_CASE.label: Use Case
USE_CASE.legalText: Data usage is restricted to the selected use cases.

END_DATE.label: End date
END_DATE.description: Validity ends on the given date.
END_DATE.legalText: The usage right expires on the given date.

FRAMEWORK_AGREEMENT.label: Framework agreement
FRAMEWORK_AGREEMENT.description: Agreement to the framework contract required.
FRAMEWORK_AGREEMENT.legalText: The consumer must have agreed to the DataExchangeGovernance framework agreement.

legalDescription.unrestricted: This policy contains no restrictions. Access to and usage of the provided data is permitted without limitation.
legalDescription.introAccess: Access to the provided data is only permitted under the following conditions:
legalDescription.introContract: Usage of the provided data is only permitted under the following conditions:

clause.useCase: The data may only be processed within the scope of the following use cases: {{list}}. Any usage for other purposes is prohibited.
clause.endDate: The granted permission is time-limited and expires automatically on {{date}}. After this date, any further usage of the data is prohibited.
clause.frameworkAgreement: The data consumer must have explicitly agreed to the framework agreement "{{agreement}}" before any data transfer may take place.

validation.policyIdTooLong: Policy ID must be at most 200 characters long
validation.constraintNotAllowedInCategory: This constraint is not allowed for the selected category.
validation.useCaseRequired: At least one use case must be selected
validation.endDateInPast: End date must be in the future

deleteDialog.title: Delete policy
deleteDialog.messageBefore: Do you really want to delete the policy
